sections.paragraph:
ὑψηλός, ή, όν [ὕψος]

—1. ‘positioned at a point that is upward’, high Mt 4:8; 17:1; Mk 9:2; Hb 1:3; Rv 21:10, 12; of arm uplifted in display of power Ac 13:17. Comp. ὑψηλότερος Hb 7:26.

—2. ‘considered to be of special importance’, as nt. noun someth. highly valued/esteemed, someth. aggrandizing, w. focus on that which appears to offer special status or sense of self-importance Lk 16:15; w. φρονεῖν Ro 11:20; 12:16; 1 Ti 6:17 v.l.
